rationale: The cited paper is used in the body of the article as a methodological precedent for the long-run time-series workflow (unit-root testing, possible cointegration, and VAR/impulse-response analysis), not just in a background list. It is also invoked in the discussion/conclusion to support interpretation about differences between mature and emerging categories in the likelihood of permanent marketing effects. Although the citing paper does not adopt the cited paper's specific share-distribution mechanism, the citation meaningfully supports both method justification and framing of findings.
relation_description: The citing paper applies methodological precedent from Bronnenberg et al. (2000) to study promotion effects on incidence, choice, and quantity using dynamic time-series tools. It also uses the cited paper's emerging-category perspective to contextualize why permanent effects may be less common in the mature categories analyzed here.
